title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:
View this email in your browser

Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

We're excited to bring back The Brew Shop 6 Roses of Spring! Nothing hails the beginning of warm weather quite like Rose and we're here to help you ring it in with 6 fresh Roses. Available in limited quantities by pre-order, make sure you grab yours before they sell out!

Our 6 Roses of Spring will be hand-selected by your favorite winos (Beth, Julie & Aidan) and filled with never-before-seen-in-the-shop Rose. What's included is a surprise, but each pack will contain ONE sparkler and FIVE still Roses.

6 Roses of Spring packs will be available for pick-up beginning Thursday, May 7th - just in time for Mother's Day, if that special Mom in your life adores Rose!Wine Club Member? Remember to put in your special code at check out for 10% off!

Join us this Friday from 5-7 PM as we welcome Liz from Williams Corner back to the shop to pour some Spring-forward wines for our free tasting! Scroll down to see our full tasting lineup for April!

🐻 Fresh hops from Mortalis
• Gaia Motuere DIPA with Citra, Mosaic & Motuere
• OldManBearMa Nelson DIPA with Citra, Simcoe & Nelson
• Artemis Pineapple Pale Ale with Citra, Galaxy & Columbus, conditioned on pineapple

🔥 Hot collabs from RAR

• Personal Plague Hazy DIPA with Citra, Riwaka, Motueka & NZ Cascade - collab with Brujos
• Eternal Prankster Hazy DIPA with Nelson, Galaxy & Eclipse - collab with Brujos

👊 Pumped for fresh Wayfinder

🍕 Can’t knock the Dissolvr drop

• Bones & Waves DDH Hazy IPA with Columbus, Hallertau Blanc, Vic Secret, Strata & El Dorado Cryo

🥜 It’s a PB&J off!
• Dissolvr PB & Jelly Engine Gluten Free Sour with Peanuts, Concord Grape & Cheesecake - collab with Xul
• Xul PB&J Mixtape Peanut Butter & Jelly Gluten Free Sour

🎸 Heavy hitters with 3 Floyds
• Cocomungo BA Imperial Stout with toasted coconut & maple syrup
• Dreadnaught West Coast DIPA
• WigSplitter Oatmeal Stout with espresso

🐆 Two returning favorites from Commonwealth
• Mano del Puma Mexican Lager
• A Tribe Called Zest Sour IPA with Tangerine, Lemon, Blood Orange & Grapefruit Zest

🌫️ Hazies from Nepenthe
• Beebweepa Hazy IPA with Citra, Mosaic & Amarillo
• Enigmatic Monolith Hazy DIPA with Citra, Galaxy, Strata & Enigma

🦈 Aslin freshies
• Maybe Shark IPA with Citra
• Daddy Juice Sour with blueberry, mango & vanilla

✨ That’s the tea, sis!
• Yoju Hard Matcha Latte Strawberry
• Lost Boy Tee Time Cider with lemons & Earl Grey tea

🍊 A dash of fruit!
• Three Notch’d Passionfruit Gose
• Shiner Raspberry Lemonade Shandy

🫰 Crispy newbies

🍃 Get those hops

🦸 N/A Heros!

• Best Day Wild American Pils

Halfway Crooks Farina

Precarious 70’s Street Munchies POG Sour

Shabo Primo Secco Brut White: Shabo wines from Ukraine have taken the shop by storm! Check out Beths’ wine write-up, below, for the details on Shabo's sparkler.

Domaine Joseph Cattin Cremant d'Alsace Brut: Now operated by the 12th generation, Cattin is the oldest family-owed and operated estate in Alsace. I have a not-so-secret love for Alsace wines with their plush texture, which becomes even more spectacular in bubble form. Made in the traditional method, Cattin Cremant is golden with apple, flowers, brioche and a creamy mousse-like texture. Vegan & Sustainable.

Lauverjat Sancerre Rose: The Lauverjat family has been growing grapes in Sancerre for generations in the tiny village of Sury-en-Vaux. Today the estate is run by husband-wife team Christian and Karine along with their son Kevin. Made sustainably with 100% hand-harvested Pinot Noir, Lauverjat Sancerre Rose is the epitome of elegant. Refreshing and dry with notes of wild strawberry, crunchy red apple, raspberry and thirst-quenching minerality.

Hermanos Senorio de P. Pecina Crianza Rioja: A top notch Rioja from Pedro Pecina, who managed the famed La Rioja Alta before starting his own project in 1992. This Crianza is the very best kind of monster - full bodied, deep fruit flavor of red and dark berries, clove, tobacco, and spice sit on top of smooth tannins and wonderfully balanced acidity. Native yeast, unfiltered, unfined.

If you're a member of our Wine Club, you've already had a taste of Shabo Winery from Odesa, Ukraine. We happened upon Shabo Telti-Kuruk still white wine and fell in love with the wine and the magical story of how Shabo wines made their way to the US. Since then, we've had the privilege of tasting through more Shabo wines with importer Sam Lerman (keep reading to hear his incredible story) and we're excited to bring Shabo Primo Secco Brut White to the shelf. For those of you new to Shabo wine, let me fill you in on the extraordinary story.

Shabo Winery was founded in 1822, although wine in Ukraine dates back to the Phoenician era. It was originally founded by Swiss natives until the Bolscheviks nationalized the winery in the 1920s. As you can imagine, the winery stagnated under Soviet rule and, in 2002, the independent Ukrainian government put the winery up for auction where it was bought by the Iukuridze family who modernized the facility and brought in winemaking help from Bordeaux. 

Shabo Wines are imported by Sam Lerman who served in the US Air Force with multiple tours in Afghanistan before pivoting to strategic defense planning at the Pentagon. In 2022, after the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, Sam went to Ukraine for some unpaid “advisory work.”  As Sam described it to us - think traveling with a pile of gold and cash paired with a Ukrainian safe house with 3 cell phones and an AK-74. It was in that safe house that Sam fell in love with the people, the mission and the wine of Ukraine. Disappointed at the lack of Ukrainian wine in the US, on future trips Sam crammed his suitcase with wine! At one point, he was stopped at the Poland border for bringing in well past the legal limit of allowable alcohol (whoops, he had 40 bottles in his bag). And that was the birth of his side gig as a wine importer. 

Shabo Primo Secco Brut White is made with the native grape Telti-Kuruk. Telti-Kuruk was originally planted during the Ottoman Empire and exists only in Ukraine and, even more special, Shabo is the only winery to grow Telti-Kuruk on ungrafted rootstock. When Sam popped open a bottle of Primo Secco, it was love at first sip for us. Made in the Charmat method, Primo Secco is layered and lively with peach, citrus fruit and pith, and a brioche finish. A perfect bottle to distract yourself from the news enjoy on the patio to welcome Spring. 

If you can believe it, in such a short time Sam is now the largest importer of Ukranian wine in the US. He's also local to the DC Metro! Expect to see more Shabo wines rotating on our shelves in the near future and, if you're a vodka fan, find your way to the nearest ABC store for some Ukrainian vodkas Sam has imported into the US.

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Beth.

April Tasting Events are spring ready!

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
